articles.title: Moscow hit by largest Ukrainian attack since start of Russia's full-scale war
articles.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre were burning after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

articles.title: Hegseth says some Nato allies will fail as he announces US review of Europe forces
articles.summary: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

articles.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. articles.title: Bird flu kills more than 75% of baby seals on remote Australian island, study finds
articles.summary: Scientists say 13,000 southern elephant seal pups on Heard Island have died of the H5N1 strain.

articles.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
articles.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

articles.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligence (AI) provider keys. "Every plugin poses as an AI coding assistant built on DeepSeek and other large language models, offering chat, commit messages, code review, bug finding, and unit tests,"

articles.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
articles.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

articles.title: First Russian shadow fleet tanker enters Channel since Smyrtos boarding
articles.summary: Forwarder, a Russian-flagged ship which left port in Primorsk last week, entered the Channel on Wednesday evening.

articles.title: Gunfire and explosions heard at Niger capital's airport
articles.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

articles.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
articles.summary: The head of the UN’s atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

articles.title: Rights defender killings hit record high as UN pushes to shore up humanitarian action
articles.summary: Attacks against human right defenders have reached record levels over the past year, according to a new report issued on Wednesday by the UN human rights office, OHCHR.

articles.title: UN urges adherence to mine ban treaty
articles.summary: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Tuesday.

articles.title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features a galaxy cluster, called CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, that is very bright at X-ray wavelengths and is one of the most extensively studied clusters at X-ray and radio wavelengths. The X-ray observations of this cluster revealed that it is two clusters merging along our line of sight. Researchers […]

articles.title: NASA Announces Public-Private Partnership to Advance Mars Science
articles.summary: NASA Wednesday announced a new public‑private partnership to advance Mars science by combining the agency’s scientific leadership with commercial innovation. Under this model, NASA will provide the Aeolus atmospheric‑science instrument payload suite, while Relativity Space supplies the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations necessary to deliver the instruments to Mars. This partnership reflects NASA’s growing commitment […]

articles.title: NASA’s Fermi Mission Uncovers Possible Sibling Supernova Remnants
articles.summary: A new study of two supernova remnants, the debris left behind after stars explode, suggests the explosions came from stellar siblings that once orbited each other. The first star’s detonation sent its binary companion hurtling through space, and then, after traveling for thousands of years, the surviving star blew up too.

articles.title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
articles.summary: Looking somewhat like a swarm of bees returning to their hive, this NASA Hubble Space Telescope image released on June 12, 2026, features the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211. Galaxy clusters like MACS0329-0211 are important signposts in the story of how the structure of the universe evolved, and are the ultimate telescopic lenses, placing gravitationally lensed galaxies from the […]

articles.title: The Download: a reality check for geoengineering and the science of interoception
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hacking the atmosphere: geoengineering gets a reality check Solar geoengineering, the controversial idea that we could deliberately intervene in the climate system to counteract global warming, is moving beyond computer simulations…

articles.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
articles.summary: An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research. articles.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
articles.summary: A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials. Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end. Before his command-and-control server went dark, he installed OpenSSH and Tailscale on a victim's machine, building a way back in that did not run through the C2 at all. articles.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
articles.summary: For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain. The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must decide which findings warrant action while operating under constant pressure and incomplete information. Increasingly, the challenge is not discovering potential risks. It is determining which risks

articles.title: 145 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
articles.summary: As many as 145 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have been compromised as part of a software supply chain attack codenamed easy-day-js, per findings from Endor Labs, JFrog, OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Synk. articles.title: Q&A: What do China’s provincial five-year plans say about climate and energy?
articles.summary: China’s provincial-level governments have now all published their 15th five-year plans – economic and social... The post Q&A: What do China’s provincial five-year plans say about climate and energy? appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
articles.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

articles.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
articles.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

articles.title: Simpler, older version of Stonehenge found three miles from famous site
articles.summary: The structure consisted of two posts that lined up with the solstices 5,000 years ago.

articles.title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
articles.summary: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.

articles.title: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
articles.summary: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.

articles.title: Search for Hidden Cosmic Companions in Sun’s Backyard
articles.summary: Some stars have planets. Others are orbited by brown dwarfs, balls of gas too massive to be planets, but too low-mass to be stars. Astronomers love these brown dwarf-star pairs because being paired with a star helps reveal a brown dwarf’s age. Ages of astronomical objects are often hard to measure, but essential for understanding […]

articles.title: Exclusive eBook: How AI is becoming the next military advisor
articles.summary: A collection of stories about how militaries are using AI models to make decisions. This subscriber-only eBook is a package of six stories that were originally published in MIT Technology Review between April 11, 2025, and April 21, 2026, and have been updated to reflect recent developments. articles.summary: If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it? articles.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
articles.summary: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. articles.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. articles.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
articles.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

articles.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain
articles.summary: Authorities in Catalonia confirmed that the 63-year-old died in the Palau de Noguera area on Wednesday.

articles.title: ‘Darkest chapter’: Record child violations in 2025, with national forces leading the way
articles.summary: For the first time, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups – and 2025 set a grim new record for the total number of child victims.

articles.title: Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’
articles.summary: As online platforms continue to fuel a surge in real-world violence against vulnerable communities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres is warning that freedom of expression must never be used to justify hate speech.

articles.title: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
articles.summary: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

articles.title: Monica Juma: Steering global cooperation at a time of converging threats
articles.summary: Criminal networks are moving faster, reaching further and blurring the lines between threats. Into this shifting landscape steps Monica Juma, taking on one of the UN’s most demanding security and justice portfolios.

articles.title: NASA Webb, Hubble Reveal History of Relic of Milky Way’s Formation
articles.summary: Researchers using two of humanity’s most powerful observatories — NASA’s James Webb and Hubble Space Telescopes — have definitively shown that Terzan 5 is not a globular star cluster as it was once classified, offering new insight into how galaxies like our own form and evolve over time. articles.title: Entrepreneurs in Nairobi make the case for going solar
articles.summary: Most of Kenya’s power grid runs on renewables. articles.title: Hacking the atmosphere: Geoengineering gets a reality check
articles.summary: Jim Franke pulls away the cover page of a presentation on the wraparound desk in his office, revealing an illustration of an odd-­looking aircraft with massive wings stretching out from a stubby fuselage.
